advisory.title: ASA-2025-004: Non-deterministic JSON Unmarshalling of IBC Acknowledgement can result in a chain halt
advisory.description: ibc-go advisory ASA-2025-004 covers a non-deterministic JSON unmarshalling bug in IBC acknowledgement processing in the transfer module. Different nodes can derive different acknowledgement bytes from the same packet, producing non-determinism that can halt the chain. Any party that can open an IBC channel can introduce the failing state. The fix in v7.9.2 and v8.6.1 restricts acknowledgement decoding in modules/apps/transfer/ibc_module.go to deterministic parsing.

timeline.notes: vulnerableHead is the v8.6.0 release commit, the last vulnerable release on the v8.x line before the v8.6.1 patch. The introducing commit is the commit that split the transfer module into its current AppModule/IBCModule layout in modules/apps/transfer/ibc_module.go, the file the v8.6.0..v8.6.1 patch modifies to deterministically decode acknowledgements.

expectedOutcome.description: Scanner should flag non-deterministic JSON unmarshalling of IBC acknowledgements in modules/apps/transfer/ibc_module.go that can produce divergent results across nodes and halt the chain.

checks.name: advisory_published
checks.details: ASA-2025-004 (GHSA-jg6f-48ff-5xrw) was published on 2025-02-27 with patches in ibc-go v7.9.2 and v8.6.1.

checks.name: vulnerable_head_in_advisory_range
checks.details: vulnerableHead is the v8.6.0 release commit, the latest tag in the >=8.0.0,<=8.6.0 vulnerable range.
